title: Redis Usage Guide for Features
description: Comprehensive guide on how features use Redis for state management, queuing, and inter-service communication in the Tahakom Data Transfer System

redis_overview.purpose: Redis serves as the backbone for state management, task queuing, and real-time communication
redis_overview.connection_pattern: Dual connections per service (data operations + pub/sub)

connection_setup.data_client: const redis = new Redis({ host: config.redis.host, port: config.redis.port, retryStrategy: (times) => Math.min(times * 50, 2000) });
connection_setup.pubsub_client: const redisPubSub = new Redis({ host: config.redis.host, port: config.redis.port, retryStrategy: (times) => Math.min(times * 50, 2000) });

redis_key_store.purpose: Centralized key management to prevent conflicts

automatic_transfer_feature.description: Uses Redis for queue-based file transfer processing and state coordination

workflow.4: autoTransferRoutes queries database and queues files
workflow.6: Results published back to result queue

drive_monitoring_feature.description: Real-time drive state monitoring and propagation

state_consumption.message_processing: Update local state variables based on drive connectivity and usage

configuration_management_feature.description: Centralized configuration state management with file watching

trigger_mechanisms.manual_trigger: Via API calls that modify config file

process_monitoring_feature.description: System process monitoring with Redis-based state distribution

data_distribution.websocket_updates: Subscribe to PROCESS_MONITOR_UPDATE for real-time updates

connected_devices_feature.description: External device monitoring with database persistence and Redis updates

real_time_updates.websocket_broadcast: Send device updates to all connected WebSocket clients

integration_with_database.persistence: Device connections stored in device_connections table
integration_with_database.state_sync: Redis used for real-time state, PostgreSQL for historical data

basic_operations.get_data: const data = await redis.get(key); return data ? JSON.parse(data) : null

connection_pooling.pattern: One connection per service for data operations
connection_pooling.reason: Pub/sub connections are blocking and should be isolated

retry_strategy.failure_handling: Graceful degradation when Redis is unavailable

memory_management.data_size_limits: Large data sets stored in PostgreSQL

connection_errors.retry_logic: Built-in retry strategy with exponential backoff

serialization_errors.invalid_data: Default values returned for invalid data
serialization_errors.error_propagation: Errors logged but not propagated to prevent cascading failures

pub_sub_errors.message_processing: Individual message failures don't stop processing
pub_sub_errors.channel_isolation: Errors in one channel don't affect others

key_management.centralized_keys: Use redisKeyStore.js for all key definitions
key_management.no_hardcoded_keys: Always import keys from central store

connection_management.dual_connections: Separate connections for data and pub/sub operations
connection_management.graceful_shutdown: Implement proper cleanup on service shutdown

message_design.channel_naming: Use '_update' suffix for state change notifications
message_design.message_versioning: Consider message versioning for future compatibility

testing.unit_tests: Mock Redis operations for unit testing
testing.integration_tests: Use test Redis instance for integration testing

logging_strategies.error_context: Include context information in error logs

redis_server_setup.installation: Redis server must be installed and running
redis_server_setup.configuration: Configure Redis server for production use

network_configuration.network_latency: Consider network latency for Redis operations

backup_and_recovery.data_persistence: Most critical data is persisted in PostgreSQL
backup_and_recovery.redis_backups: Redis primarily used for transient state
backup_and_recovery.disaster_recovery: Services can restart with default state

connection_issues.solution: Check Redis server status, verify network connectivity, review retry strategy
connection_issues.prevention: Implement proper error handling and retry logic

queue_blockages.solution: Clear blocked queues, restart consumer services, check for processing errors
queue_blockages.prevention: Implement queue monitoring and automatic cleanup

pub_sub_delays.solution: Check Redis server performance, verify subscription setup, review message processing
pub_sub_delays.prevention: Monitor pub/sub performance and implement timeouts

memory_issues.solution: Implement key expiration, clean up old data, optimize data structures
memory_issues.prevention: Regular monitoring and data cleanup procedures

recommended_improvements: Implement key expiration for temporary data
Add Redis cluster support for high availability
Implement message acknowledgment for critical queues
Add Redis metrics collection and monitoring
Implement connection pooling for better performance
Add data compression for large messages
Implement circuit breaker pattern for Redis operations
scalability_considerations: Consider Redis Cluster for horizontal scaling
Implement queue partitioning for high-throughput scenarios
Add load balancing for multiple Redis instances
Consider Redis Streams for advanced messaging patterns
